runner: manual: cross-compile vulkan_verify (NDK x86_64-linux-android28-clang++, glslc) -> adb push -> adb shell ELIZA_ALLOW_SOFTWARE_VULKAN=1 ./vulkan_verify against the Cuttlefish (cvd-1) SwiftShader Vulkan ICD
sourceOfTruth: Cuttlefish's virtio-gpu Vulkan ICD here is SwiftShader (LLVM 16.0.0, vendor 0x1ae0 = Google software rasterizer; api=1.3). Per the kernel-contract.json fail-closed evidence rule, a software Vulkan ICD CANNOT produce recordable runtime-ready evidence. All eight standalone fixture cases pass numerically; they are recorded here as diagnostic-only to document the Android x86_64 cross-build is well-formed and the SPIR-V shaders load + dispatch correctly through the standard Android Vulkan loader. Recordable Android x86_64 Vulkan evidence requires real ChromeOS x86_64 GPU silicon (Adreno/Mali under ARCVM) or a passed-through host GPU via crosvm gfxstream.

host.kernel: Linux localhost 6.12.38-android16-5-g501f58f107ab-ab13974591 #1 SMP PREEMPT Thu Aug 21 21:28:12 UTC 2025 x86_64 Toybox
host.vulkanIcdSource: adb shell cmd gpu vkjson | first device .properties.deviceName

caveats: Numeric correctness only — the SPIR-V binaries are well-formed and the shaders execute correctly under a Vulkan 1.3 loader on Android x86_64. The fixtures pass.

NOT recordable runtime-ready evidence. SwiftShader is a software rasterizer; per kernel-contract.json fail-closed hosts rule (and the vulkan_dispatch_smoke / vulkan-native-smoke / android-vulkan-smoke device-class screens), software ICDs are diagnostic-only.

PLATFORM_MATRIX.md's android-x86_64-vulkan row stays authored-pending-hardware for graph dispatch; that needs real ChromeOS x86_64 GPU silicon (Adreno/Mali under ARCVM) or a passed-through host GPU via crosvm gfxstream + a real-GPU host.
